LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS BEYONDCHRON AND RANDY SHAW'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT [C.C.P. SECTION 425.16] - GRANTED, NO OPPOSITION FILED. PLAINTIFF CANNOT AVOID AN ANTI-SLAPP MOTION BY AMENDING THE COMPLAINT PRIOR TO THE HEARING ON THE MOTION. PLAINTIFF HAS SUBMITTED NO EVIDENCE DEMONSTRATING A PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING IF ALLOWED TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT. (SEE LAM V. CAO (2009) 171 C.A. 4TH 858). Off Calendar Sep-16-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS BEYONDCHRON AND RANDY SHAW'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT [C.C.P. SECTION 425.16].

APPEARANCES: Rita O'Flynn, 260-7608 pltf & opposing party in propria persona; Matt McFarland, Esq. of Tenderloin Housing Clinic 771-9850 for defts & moving parties.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - granted, no opposition filed. Plaintiff cannot avoid an anti-SLAPP motion by amending the complaint prior to the hearing on the motion. Plaintiff has submitted no evidence demonstrating a probability of prevailing if allowed to amend tHE complaint. (See Lam v. Cao (2009) 171 C.A. 4th 858). Plaintiff's request for continuance to respond to motion was denied. Order signed in open court. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
